title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host network namespace

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host network namespace
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the hostNetwork flag set to true.
input_row.rationale: A container running in the host's network namespace could access the local loopback device, and could access network traffic to and from other pods. There should be at least one admission control policy defined which does not permit containers to share the host network namespace. If you need to run containers which require access to the host's network namesapces, this should be defined in a separate policy and you should carefully check to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to use that policy. Impact: Pods defined with spec.hostNetwork: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.
input_row.audit: To fetch hostNetwork from each pod. get pods -A -o=jsonpath=$'{range .items[*]}{@.metadata.name}: {@.spec.hostNetwork}\n{end}'
input_row.remediation: Add policies to each namespace in the cluster which has user workloads to restrict the admission of hostNetwork containers. Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on the creation of hostNetwork containers. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/
input_row.impact: Pods defined with spec.hostNetwork: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_host_network_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all namespaces in the cluster.
- For each namespace, check for the presence and enforcement of Pod Security Admission (PSA) policies or legacy PodSecurityPolicies (if still in use) that restrict the use of `spec.hostNetwork: true` in pod specs.
- Use the Kubernetes API or kubectl to:
  - List all pods in all namespaces and check if any have `spec.hostNetwork: true`.
    - Command: `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o jsonpath="{range .items[*]}{.metadata.namespace}:{.metadata.name}:{.spec.hostNetwork}{'\n'}{end}"`
  - List and inspect Pod Security Admission labels on each namespace:
    - Command: `kubectl get ns --show-labels`
    - Look for labels such as `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted` or `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version=latest`, which by default disallow `hostNetwork: true`.
  - If using PodSecurityPolicies (PSP, deprecated in 1.25+), check for policies that restrict `hostNetwork: true` and ensure only specific service accounts or users are allowed exceptions.
    - Command: `kubectl get psp -o yaml` and inspect `spec.hostNetwork` field.
- Pass Criteria:
  - All namespaces with user workloads have Pod Security Admission set to at least `restricted` level, or equivalent policies, which prevent pods from using `hostNetwork: true` unless explicitly allowed for tightly controlled service accounts.
  - No pods are running with `spec.hostNetwork: true` except those in namespaces or with service accounts explicitly permitted by policy.
- Fail Criteria:
  - Any namespace with user workloads lacks a restrictive PSA policy or equivalent, or allows unrestricted use of `hostNetwork: true`.
  - Pods with `spec.hostNetwork: true` are found outside of explicitly permitted contexts.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.10 PDF
